text:
25 Tips On How To Hire Tech Talent

Key Challenges in Tech Recruitment: high salaries (developers earn $100-140k annually), rapidly evolving technical skills, intense competition for talent, unconscious hiring bias, and lengthy 44-day average hiring processes that cause top candidates to accept offers elsewhere.

Core Hiring Strategies: The 25 recommendations span recruitment fundamentals through organizational culture. Early tips emphasize precision job descriptions, talent sourcing through referrals and developer communities like GitHub, and understanding candidate expectations beyond compensation.

Mid-section advice focuses on process improvements: streamlining technical hiring, implementing real-world assessments, and offering excellent candidate experiences. The article stresses that 'candidates will have 44 days from the time they apply until they sign a contract to schedule interviews and maybe receive offers for other technology-related roles,' highlighting urgency.

Distinctive Approaches: Notable recommendations include organizing virtual job fairs, connecting with coding bootcamps, campus recruitment for junior talent, and hiring freelance technical recruiters who possess specialized network access. The article advocates hiring based on problem-solving abilities rather than requiring computer science degrees, noting this 'brings diversity in the workplace leading to a productive workplace.'

WeCP offers AI-powered assessment tools and interview platforms designed to reduce hiring friction while maintaining evaluation rigor through features detecting candidate fraud and measuring AI-augmented technical capabilities.
